review_title: Excellent Product for MacOS Device Management *****
description: I would recommend this product to any company using MacOS. We moved from using Intune from MacOS devices to Kandji with a focus on security and have not looked back. The Onboarding was exceptional with knowledgeable staff able to assist us with our initial blueprints and configurations. When things have not worked or we have required support the support team and really responsive and resolve issues fast. Overall this is a product i would not be without.
author: Richard M.
pros: Simplified the Process of Management of MacOS devices for a team that was more windows based. Onboarding was really quick and easily with excellent hands on support. Support response and resolution times are really good too. The whole product is intuitive and easy to use.
cons: Alerts and clearing alerts is a pain. We see some alerting for some devices where through for example apps are already deployed before onboarding and the software can not be installed. It would be really nice if these could be cleared easily or "ignored" in the UI.

review_title: all in all, it´s very good
description: My overall experience with Iru has been very positive. I use it frequently and enjoy working with it. The interface is clear and easy to understand, which keeps the learning curve low. Value for money is solid given the quality of the product and the responsive customer support. The ease of use is one of its strongest points, and device and client management features work reliably. The main area for improvement remains the activity/audit log and its filtering capabilities.
author: Jamie K.
pros: What I like most about Iru is the intuitive user interface, which makes daily work straightforward and efficient. On top of that, the support team is impressively responsive and knowledgeable — issues get resolved quickly, which is a huge plus.
cons: The activity log remains a significant weak point. It is very difficult to search through — there is no simple way to filter by keyword or by user. For example, if an admin performs an action in the console, you often cannot find any trace of it in the activity view. This makes auditing and troubleshooting unnecessarily cumbersome.

review_title: Mandatory Review
description: Contract management continues to be messy, often incorrect, late renewal notices, incorrect quotes, etc.
author: Ester C.
pros: Iru provides comprehensive endpoint protection with broad coverage across devices, helping reduce risk and centralize security management.
cons: Contract management is consistently problematic, with frequent inaccuracies, delayed renewal notices, and incorrect quotes that require follow-up.

review_title: Simple to use, deeply capable, and the best value for dollar I get period as an IT Manager
description: Exceptionally positive. From first onboarding, their support and engineering team have been reachable, knowledgeable, and so so kind and helpful. Their product team is innovative, and are adding the features that I as am administrator want to see. And when they do roll out totally new functionality like they have done somewhat recently with EDR, with vulnerability management, and with cross-platform device management - it is EASY to show my executive team why moving to Iru for those needs is the right choice.
author: Topher O.
pros: The first thing that drew my organization is was an MDM provider that is focused first and foremost on supporting our Apple-based ecosystem of devices - and Iru from day one has excelled at that. But as time has gone on and we have remained extremely happy customers, the platform has expanded to meet not just our Apple device management needs, but now cover our EDR needs, our vulnerability reporting, management for the few windows and android devices in our fleet - all while remaining easy to use, easy to navigate to where we need to go quickly, and constantly adding new quality of life features. They are committed to growth, not just into new sectors or categories - but to growing the product to make my job as an administrator using their tools easier and more effective.
cons: give me D A R K M O D E please. I love your platform and the only thing that would make me love it more would be avoiding the flash bang of loading into my Iru dash from other white-on-black interfaces

review_title: One of the Most Intuitive MDM Solutions—Up and Running in Days
description: We recently rolled out IRU, as our Mobile Device Management solution for Apple devices. During our evaluation process, we reviewed several platforms including Microsoft Intune, IRU, and Jamf. After careful consideration, IRU stood out as the solution that delivered the full range of functionality we required, while remaining within an affordable price range. From both a senior and junior engineer perspective, the platform is intuitive and straightforward to configure and manage. The setup experience was smooth, and the interface makes day to day administration simple without sacrificing depth or control. One of the key strengths for us was the ability to assign permissions based on our RBAC roles, allowing appropriate access across the team while maintaining governance and security. From an administrator standpoint, the visibility and control are excellent. From an end user perspective, the experience is seamless and minimally disruptive. We were able to complete the full onboarding process for all devices and users within a month, covering configuration, testing, and full rollout. Overall, IRU has proven to be a reliable, scalable, and cost effective MDM solution. I would highly recommend this platform to any company looking to manage their Apple device fleet efficiently and confidently.
author: Chaz V.
pros: This is one of the most intuitive MDM solutions I’ve ever used. Unlike other MDM solutions, it required very little training, and we were up and running in a matter of days.
cons: It would be much better if the Threats and Vulnerability modules were included in the main package, rather than offered only as additional add-on modules.

review_title: Great macOS MDM solution for businesses.
description: I was looking for a good MDM solution to manage all my macOS devices and Iru does a fantastic job. It allows me to manage the devices, deploy and enforce applications, apply group policies, wipe, DEP, and bypass any personal end user activation locks. Setup and deployment is very easy and pretty easy to learn to administor. UI is clean, modern, and easy to navigate.
author: David N.
pros: The overall UI interface is simple and modern. Enrolling macOS devices either manually or through ABM/ADE/DEP is very straightforward and works as expected. Device management when enrolled is great. You get device details, ability to send MDM actions, and deploy apps automatically or through its self service app is very useful.
cons: You can't manually clear/mute device alerts. You have to somehow physically remediate or resolve the issue before the alert is cleared. They also charge you in batches of 25 devices/licenses only.

review_title: Great MDM for Apple products
description: I've been working for about four years, and honestly, I don't have much to complain about, but some changes, sometimes, are not so welcoming.
author: Vitor P.
pros: It is a very easy tool to use; I've never had a bad experience or downtime from the platform itself. The support team is also great.
cons: The auto app library is still not big enough. Also, some features are just unlocked if you hire the endpoint detection services. The change from blueprint to assignment map is also not great, since there are some small things you can do with blueprints that are not available on maps.

review_title: Great MDM for Apple environment
author: Washington J.
pros: How well it works with Apple environment. Great ready made features. Responsive customer support and patch management. Very good device management functions.
cons: Migration is difficult, but it was largely because of our previous MDM that controlled totally the profiles.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Simple and reliable Apple device management
description: IRU helps us centralize the management of our Apple devices and automate many tasks that would otherwise require manual configuration. This reduces the time spent on device setup, software deployment, and policy enforcement. As a result, our IT team can manage devices more efficiently and ensure that all machines stay compliant with our security and configuration standards.
author: Domingo J.
pros: IRU has been a solid solution for managing Apple devices in our organization. The platform is intuitive, easy to deploy, and the automation features make device configuration and maintenance much more efficient. The interface is clear and policies can be applied quickly, which saves a lot of time for IT teams. Documentation and resources are also helpful when setting up new configurations.
cons: That said, some advanced customization options could be a bit more flexible. Overall, it’s been a very positive experience and a good choice for organizations looking to simplify Apple device management.

review_title: Great MDM Solution
description: Iru is a lifesaver for our Mac fleet. The Auto Apps and Blueprints make deployment a breeze, as well as integrating more apps through ABM
author: Cristian P.
pros: Iru is a total lifesaver for our Mac fleet! The Auto Apps feature saves me hours on patching, and the Blueprints make deployment incredibly smooth.
cons: The alerting system a bit noisy; you can't easily bulk-ignore alerts for apps that users installed manually before onboarding. Lastly, while the 2026 rebrand to Iru added Windows/Android support, those features still feel a bit "lite" compared to the deep macOS controls we’re used to.

review_title: The Kandji effect!
description: The migration from JAMF to Kandji was a seamless transition for our team. The new platform's intuitive and user-friendly interface made it easy for everyone to adapt, leading to a very positive experience and a great value for the price! Overall, Kandji has provided a truly positive and efficient experience for our entire team.
author: Sunil K.
pros: Since we've adopted Kandji, it's become a favorite tool across our team, mainly due to its versatility and ease of use. Key Features We Appreciate: Streamlined Device Management: Actions like managing, resetting, locking, or deleting a device are straightforward and save us a significant amount of time. Powerful Integrations: The built-in API connections allow us to expand our automation capabilities, making our workflows more efficient. Excellent Customer Support: The support team is consistently responsive and helpful, which has made a huge difference for us. Efficient Blueprint System: The ability to use multiple blueprints is a game-changer. It makes updating and triggering patch management easy, and allows us to deploy specific configurations to different departments or user groups seamlessly.
cons: Nothing comes to mind, we always look forward to NEW features being added to the platform which keep things fresh.

review_title: MDM That Simplifies macOS Management
description: Our overall experience with Kandji has been very positive. The platform significantly simplified macOS management and compliance, reducing manual work through automation and pre-built controls. Deployment was smooth, and end-user experience has improved with fewer support touchpoints. Kandji's focus on Apple-first design is clear, and support from their team has been responsive and helpful. For organizations deeply invested in the Apple ecosystem, Kandji delivers strong value and operational efficiency.
author: Saranraj M.
pros: Kandji stands out for its modern and intuitive user interface, making macOS device management simple and efficient even for teams with limited Apple expertise. The pre-built library of security and configuration profiles (Auto Apps, Parameters, Blueprints) significantly reduces setup time and ensures compliance with industry standards out-of-the-box. The platform’s automation capabilities—such as auto-remediation, zero-touch deployment, and seamless integration with Apple Business Manager—greatly enhance operational efficiency. Kandji also provides excellent visibility into device health and compliance status through real-time dashboards and reports. Overall, it’s a well-designed solution built with the Apple ecosystem in mind.
cons: While Kandji offers a polished and user-friendly experience, it currently lacks support for managing non-Apple devices, which limits its use in mixed-environment organizations.

review_title: Switching to Kandji/Iru
author: Frank W.
pros: I was first attracted the ease of use, but later also fell in love with the update and patch management structures. The customer support is outstanding and for me, the switch to Iru has been good value for money. The transition from our old device management was simple and the Iru rep assigned to me knew all about all I had to do to make it happen.
cons: I'm still finding it icky to navigate through large assignment maps, but other than that I'm a happy camper.

review_title: Great support that feels like an extension of our IT team
description: The customer support has been excellent. Often SAAS providers just offer a great pre-sales experience but fall down once you sign up. However Kandji has truly offered hands-on problem solving even in cases where we're asking them to help us solve 3rd party issues like help with custom scripting or installing unsupported 3rd party software. It truly feels like we have an extended IT team in our business thanks to Kandji support.
author: Steve W.
pros: The interface, pre-set software and configuration inclusions, built-in security blue-prints, and the truly responsive customer support make Kandji extremely easy and valuable to our business.
cons: Bundling extras like Endpoint Detection and Response and Vulnerability Management mean potentially additional costs for important security and management features, although the cost aren't too high and the features are good value.

review_title: A huge leap forward for our team when managing Apple devices
description: Kandji has been revolutionary for our team and we now manage all aspects of Apple devicement Mangement easily. The platform has ended up being great value for money and we are now confident our devices are secure and our users are happy. Features like Passport, Liftoff Blueprints all with clean and modern interface means our etams and users love using Kandji.
author: Jonathan N.
pros: Having managed MDM plaforms for 15 years, we found migrating to Kandji was a huge step forward for us and feels like a highly capable, secure and modern platform that has streamlined our Apple Device Management. Managing devices via blueprint is smart, logical and easy to understand what is going on with a device. Previous platforms have left us totally bewildered at times as to what is triggered and where and can be overly complex for a small IT team. Kandji has revolutionised this process for us and allowed us to confidently configure end to end device mangagment, onboarding flows securely and confidently. Customer support were super helpful in the migration process and was very easy tmove our whole estate in just a few days.
cons: Coming from other platforms that are very powerful with granular settings, but often difficult to use, we intitially found we had to reframe our thinking using blueprints and a lack of smart groups. However once you overcome this you realise just how powerful yet simple to manage to platform is. Auto app updates can be a bit annopying as the user is nagged frequently and this should be configurable to make the user experience more enjoyable.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Apple device management made simple with Kandji
description: The experience is overwhelmingly positive and we have reduced a lot of friction for our staff when it comes to Apple device Management
author: Dragan O.
pros: The ease of use, especially how easy it was to understand the project as a power user and how easy it was to move over from our previous provider
cons: Certain aspects differed from our previous solution that we had gotten used to in terms of management. Once we learned Kandji’s way of doing things we adapter quickly

review_title: Kandji: Effortless Apple Device Management with Modern Simplicity
description: My overall experience with Kandji has been overwhelmingly positive. As an Apple device management solution, Kandji delivers on its promise of making device setup, security, and ongoing management both efficient and user-friendly. The platform’s modern interface and automation features have significantly reduced the time and effort required to manage our fleet of Apple devices.
author: Johann D.
pros: Kandji stands out as a modern, user-friendly Apple device management solution that truly streamlines the IT experience. What I liked most about Kandji is its combination of simplicity and robust functionality. The platform’s intuitive interface makes even complex tasks, like deploying security controls or managing device configurations feel effortless. Which is a major advantage for both seasoned IT professionals and those newer to Apple administration.
cons: Kandji only supports Apple devices. Organizations with any non-Apple hardware (Android or Windows) must look elsewhere for unified device management.

review_title: Moving from the complexity of Jamf to the intuitive simplicity of Kandji
description: It's been fantastic to have migrated from Jamf to Kandji. We had a single engineer complete the task in a fairly short timeline, and this resulted in both a saving of costs and also a much more manageable MDM service for our Macs.
author: Chris N.
pros: Kandji has a brilliantly designed and user friendly interface. The simplicity and feel of the admin console makes up-skilling new admins very quick and intuitive. Navigating the UI and using the system is quick and efficient.
cons: Kandji has historically lacked some granularity and logic in management of devices. Blueprints (the mechanism of grouping apps, policies and more to apply to devices) were quite rigid. However this is being addressed now with 'new' blueprint flows which contain conditional branching logic.

review_title: Streamlining our device management - and it's beautiful!
description: Our overall experience with Kandji has been fantastic. The platform performs smoothly, the interface is a pleasure to use, and the integration with our Apple devices feels effortless. Customer support has been a standout — their AI chatbot is genuinely helpful, and when it can’t solve an issue, we get connected to a real, highly knowledgeable support person almost instantly. They’ve been consistently quick to respond and capable of resolving our questions or problems. Kanji has truly streamlined our device management processes and delivered excellent support along the way. Plus the EDR and vulnerability management give us extra piece of mind!
author: Marcel P.
pros: Kandji is incredibly seamless and intuitive to use. The learning curve is almost flat — we didn’t have to spend time learning complex processes, and everything feels very natural. The UI and UX are excellent: clean, visually appealing, and aligned with the design quality we expect from Apple devices. It’s easy to navigate, well thought out, and simply makes our lives a lot easier when managing iOS and Mac devices.
cons: The only drawback we encountered was with the pricing tiers. Initially, the structure wasn’t as transparent as we would have liked, which made it a bit tricky to figure out the best fit. However, after some discussion, we found a pricing option that worked well for us, so it was more of a minor inconvenience than an ongoing issue.

review_title: Kandji Review.
description: My overall experience with Kandji has been really positive. It fills the gaps that other MDM solutions can't cover and meets all our needs.
author: Bled S.
pros: What I really appreciate is the full range of functionality it offers for macOS devices, along with its user-friendly interface. It integrates seamlessly with our IAM system, and everything works smoothly.
cons: The reporting could definitely be improved. For example, an interactive dashboard with more detailed statistics—such as device status, installed apps, and other available information per device—would be very helpful.

review_title: Kandji is Awesome! Admin of 3+ Years!
description: Kandji has been great! I have been using it for over 3 years and I have seen functionality added to platform that has made it even more intuitive to use. Overall, I have not experienced too many limitations with Kandji; however, Kandji always seems to find a way to improve a process to make the experience better. One example being the Assignment Maps Kandji Support is also extremely supportive with providing guidance on custom solutions to accomplish something not possible out of the box.
author: Malcolm A.
pros: Highly user-friendly AND also very technical under the hood The UI is very enjoyable and easy to navigate - this theme trickles down throughout the platform. The Auto Apps feature where highly used business applications are ready to deploy without the need to create custom pkg is extremely useful and lightens the lift tremendously. On the other hand, the Custom Apps is extremely helpful for the more in-depth app deployments. Everything is there and easy to manage. Also, utilizing API for more in-depth reports and custom integrations has made the load of inventory management a lot lighter.
cons: There has not been many scenarios where I thought that Kandji cannot do this. I use Kandji every day and have not run into any frustrations or blockers.

review_title: Good experience !
description: I've been using Iru for about three years now. From the first time I used it until today, I've been delighted with the product. It's intuitive to use, the features are relevant, and the support/sales teams are always available and very helpful (even in different time zone).
author: Julien P.
pros: Ease of use, managed deployment, ease of device management, compatibility with a wide range of applications, the support.
cons: The only think I'm thinking about (and it's not what I like or not) it's the agent. Maybe it could be practical to have some more options on the device side. Sometimes when we download an app on the self service, it takes a very long time before we have it install... and it the same when we want to re-install.

review_title: Well that's easy!
description: Kandji makes Apple device management feel effortless. It’s a reliable, well-supported platform that simplifies complex workflows and lets IT teams focus on what matters. With just a few clicks, you’re up and running—exactly what you'd expect from the "easy button."
author: Joseph D.
pros: Kandji is a seamless, intuitive solution for managing Apple devices, it truly lives up to its reputation as the "easy button" for Mac MDM. From deployment to daily management, the platform is incredibly user-friendly and straightforward to implement—even for teams without deep technical expertise. One of Kandji’s standout features is its exceptional customer support. The live chat team is highly engaged, responsive, and consistently helpful. Issues are resolved quickly, and you never feel like you're talking to a bot or stuck in a queue. It’s clear that Kandji invests in real people who know their product and care about your experience.
cons: The only notable limitation is the lack of functionality to uninstall existing apps from devices. This can be a challenge when trying to remove unapproved or unwanted software that may have been installed outside of Kandji’s controls. Adding this feature would make an already excellent tool even more robust.

review_title: Solid MDM with great support!
description: I've used Kandji at two different companies now and it's been great at both. The visibility into what's happening across all our devices is excellent. The functionality covers pretty much everything we need for managing our Macs. It's made my job a lot easier and saved us a ton of time on device management tasks
author: Anthony C.
pros: The support team is honestly amazing. Whenever I've reached out they actually get back to me quickly. The whole platform is super intuitive, which is huge because I don't have to spend forever training people on it. I really appreciate how customizable everything is. We're not stuck with one size fits all settings, we can actually make it work for our specific scenario. Also, the user community in Mac Admins is really active and helpful, which has saved me more than once when I needed to figure something out.
cons: The pricing structure is a bit confusing and tier based but they work with ya. The vulnerability and threat detection features are pretty cool, but they're still fairly new so they're not quite as robust as I'd like them to be yet. I'm sure they'll get there though. One small thing that has bugged me for years haha, is that Xcode isn't in the auto apps list, which would make life easier for our devs since we have to manually handle that deployment.

review_title: Powerful and Easy to Use MDM tool!
description: Kandji helps us manage our devices to ensure our compliance and contractual obligations by providing us with easy to use controls and meaningful support. It has helped us streamline the processes around our requirements given us time back to work on other projects.
author: Tom J.
pros: In a word...simplicity. Kandji has streamlined our enrollment, usability, and ongoing administration of our devices. Support is quick to respond and very helpful. Prebuilt blueprints, like those for CIS controls, make quickly achieving a minimum level of security easy.
cons: It would be nice to be able to organize devices into groups, but I understand that tagging can achieve similar results.
